basics.name: Ashwin K M
basics.summary: Undergraduate researcher in the B.Tech Mathematics and Computing program at IISc Bangalore, working on large language models (post-training, reasoning, domain adaptation), reinforcement learning, and agentic / multi-agent AI, with a recurring interest in structured, persistent memory for AI systems.

work.summary: Investigated whether a human-inspired Learn–Master–Teach lifecycle improves LLM mathematical reasoning beyond static fine-tuning.
highlights: Designed a multi-stage pipeline: curriculum-grounded learning, interactive concept refinement, feedback-driven practice, multi-teacher distillation, self-reflection, hallucination mitigation, and pedagogical RL.
Reported +10–17 percentage-point accuracy gains over the base model on CBSE-style math benchmarks.
Built MentorX, an adaptive tutoring model deployed via the AhamX adaptive-learning platform, and piloted it with ~50 Kendriya Vidyalaya government-school students.

work.summary: Anomaly detection for electricity-consumption time series.
highlights: Built a controlled synthetic anomaly-injection framework (NNG-Mix-inspired Type 2/3/4 anomalies).
Built a reproducible benchmarking pipeline for classical anomaly detectors (IQR, LOF, Isolation Forest, Modified Z-Score) with Kalman filtering and seasonal decomposition.
Analyzed detector sensitivity under controlled anomaly regimes.

courses: Linear Algebra, Real Analysis, Probability & Statistics

education.institution: Lalaji Memorial Omega International School, Chennai (CBSE)

awards.title: World Scholar's Cup — Global Round, Manila
awards.summary: Won 8 Gold and 5 Silver medals and qualified for the Tournament of Champions at Yale University.

awards.title: National Talent Search Examination (NTSE) — Stage I Awardee
Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana (KVPY) — SA stream
awards.summary: Top ~2000 / top 4% in the SA stream.

publications.name: Learn–Master–Teach Tuning: A Human-Pedagogy Inspired Fine-Tuning Paradigm for LLMs
publications.summary: A human-pedagogy-inspired fine-tuning paradigm (learn → master → teach) that improves LLM mathematical reasoning by 10–17 percentage points over the base model. Ashwin K M et al. _Link to be added._

publications.name: FOCAL: Decoupling Conflicting Objectives in LLM Domain Adaptation via Temporal Orthogonalization
publications.summary: Decouples conflicting objectives during LLM domain adaptation via temporal orthogonalization. Ashwin K M et al. _Title/link to be confirmed._

publications_note: presentations/teaching/portfolio are shown on their own pages (/talks/, /teaching/, /portfolio/), not on the JSON CV.
